div1.entryFree:
Spy, vb. 1) intr. to look, to pry: revealing day through every cranny --es, Lucr. 1086. now will I to the chink, to s. an I can hear my Thisby's face, Mids. V, 195. I s. You s.! what do you s.? Troil. III, 1, 102 ("the usual exclamation at a childish game called Hie, spy, hie". Steevens). With into: what a man cannot smell out, he may s. into, Lr. I, 5, 24. it is my nature's plague to s. into abuses, Oth. III, 3, 147.
2) tr. to perceive, to see: like one that --es an adder, Ven. 878. she --ed the hunted boar, Ven. 878 --es the foul boar's conquest, Ven. 878 he --es Lucretia's glove, Lucr. 316. in thy shady cell, where none may s. him, sits Sin, Lucr. 316 he --ed in her some blemish, Lucr. 316 such signs of truth in his plain face she --ed, Lucr. 316 he, --ing her, bounced in, Pilgr. 83. Love . . . --ed a blossom, 229 and LLL IV, 3, 103. what is in Silvia's face, but I may s. more fresh in Julia's, Gent. V, 4, 114. I s. entertainment in her, Wiv. I, 3, 48. IV, 2, 204. IV, 6, 43. Meas. III, 2, 44. Ado II, 3, 254. Mids. III, 2, 19. V, 187. V, 187 Shr. IV, 2, 60. All's III, 5, 93. R2 II, 1, 271. H4B II, 2, 87. H6A I, 1, 127. I, 4, 19. I, 4, 19 H6B I, 1, 242. H6C III, 2, 136. IV, 6, 28. V, 3, 4. R3 I, 1, 26 (Ff see). I, 4, 270. Troil. I, 2, 153. Troil. I, 2, 153 Rom. IV, 1, 68. Tim. I, 2, 52. Lr. II, 4, 250. Oth. I, 1, 77. to s. out (== to look out in Tim. III, 2, 67): what eye but such an eye would s. out such a quarrel? Rom. III, 1, 23.
